de.name: B2B-Großhandel
de.description:
Geschäftskunden, größere Warenkörbe, Angebots- und Projektgeschäft,
Zahlungsziele + Skonto. Sales-Team + Außendienst statt Marketing-
Funnel — Beziehungspflege und Konditionspflege sind der Hebel.
Kein D2C-Stack: Mahnwesen, Bonität, Approval-Workflows und EDI
tragen das Geschäft, nicht Newsletter und CAC.

emphasis.overview:
Wer sind eure Top-30-Kunden, welcher Anteil hängt an ihnen, wie
läuft das Risk-Konzentrations-Profil? Bei Wegfall eines Top-Kunden
— wo bricht's? Frame eures B2B-Geschäfts: Sortimentsbreite vs.
-tiefe, Anteil Direkt vs. Reseller, Margen-Korridor pro
Kundenklasse.
emphasis.market:
B2B-Markt = wenige große Player, längere Sales-Cycles. Erfasst
die Hauptwettbewerber + ihre USPs (Konditionen, Sortiment,
Lieferzeit). Branchen-Marktanteil wenn relevant — Wachstum kommt
aus Kunden-Akquise + Share-of-Wallet, nicht Werbedruck.
emphasis.company:
B2B-Belegfluss ist Pflichtprogramm — Angebot → Auftrag → Lieferschein →
Rechnung. AGB-Footer, Mahnwesen-Texte, Skonto-Regeln und
Lieferbedingungen müssen sauber stehen. Reverse-Charge und
Drittland-Export sind hier Standard, nicht Ausnahme.
emphasis.products:
Listenpreis + Mengenstaffeln + Kundenpreise (Sonderkonditionen) +
Projektpreise. Marge pro Kundenklasse explizit ausweisen — wer
bekommt was zu welchem Preis und warum. Sortiments-Schnitt:
Kernsortiment (immer auf Lager) vs. Spezial (Bestellware) vs.
Drop-Ship.
emphasis.masterdata:
Stammdaten sind euer Geschäftskern — Kundengruppen + Preislisten +
Konditionen + Kreditlimits + Zahlungsziele. Sauber gepflegte
Kundenklassen sind die Voraussetzung für korrekte Pricing- und
Mahnautomatik.
emphasis.channels:
Anteil Direktvertrieb (Außendienst) vs. Innendienst vs. optionaler
B2B-Shop. KEIN Marktplatz-Spiel. EDI-Anbindungen für Top-Kunden
wenn relevant (EDIFACT, OpenTrans, REST).
emphasis.processes:
Quote-to-Cash mit Approval-Workflow für Sonderpreise und
Kreditlimit-Überschreitungen. SLA pro Auftragsart (Eilauftrag,
Lagerauftrag, Streckengeschäft). Reklamationsprozess statt
Returns-Portal.
emphasis.payment:
Zahlungsziele 14/30/60/90 Tage netto, Skonto-Regeln, Vorkasse-
Schwellen für Neukunden, Kreditlimit pro Kunde + automatische
Sperre bei Überschreitung. SEPA-Lastschrift wenn Mandate
vorliegen.
emphasis.accounting:
Mahnwesen + OPOS-Auswertung sind Tagesgeschäft. Pflichten:
ZM-Meldung monatlich für EU-B2B (Reverse-Charge), Drittland-
Ausfuhrnachweise, USt-Voranmeldung. Faktoring oder
Warenkreditversicherung als Risikohedge prüfen.
emphasis.roles:
Approval-Workflow für Sonderpreise und Kreditlimit-Erhöhungen.
Außendienst sieht nur eigene Kunden + Provisionsbasis. 4-Augen-
Prinzip ab Schwellenwerten.
emphasis.custom_agents:
Mahnstufen automatisiert + zeitlich getriggert, OPOS-Reports an
Buchhaltung wöchentlich, Kreditlimit-Check vor Auftragsfreigabe,
Provisions-Abrechnung monatlich.
emphasis.logistics:
Mix aus Paket (Carrier-API) und Spedition (Pallet, FTL). Pro Auftrag
Fracht-Logik. Lager-Picking eher batch/wave bei Großmengen, weniger
Single-Order. Streckengeschäft als Bypass.
emphasis.systems:
ERP als zentrales System — angebunden an Buchhaltung (DATEV),
ggf. PIM, ggf. CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive), EDI-Provider
und Bonitätsdienst (Creditreform).
emphasis.financials:
Kunden-Konzentrations-Risiko + DSO + OPOS-Stand sind die KPIs.
Margenwasserfall pro Kundenklasse. Cash-Flow-Forecast aus
Zahlungszielen + erwarteten Skonto-Abzügen.
emphasis.reporting:
Top-30-Kunden-Report, OPOS, DSO, Mahnstufen-Pipeline, Provisions-
Auswertung. Pro Außendienst-Mitarbeiter eigene Sicht auf seine
Kunden + Pipeline.
emphasis.migration:
Cutover-Plan mit Top-Kunden zuerst → mittlere Kunden → Long-Tail.
EDI-Mappings vor Go-Live testen. Bestände sauber überleiten,
damit Kreditlimit-Check ab Tag 1 stimmt.

en.name: B2B Wholesale
en.description:
Business customers, larger baskets, quote-and-project business,
payment terms + early-payment discount. Sales team + field reps
instead of marketing funnel — relationship and terms management
are the lever. Not a D2C stack: dunning, credit checks, approval
workflows and EDI run the business, not newsletters and CAC.

emphasis.overview:
Who are your top-30 customers, what share rides on them, what
does the risk-concentration profile look like? If a top customer
churns — where does it break? Frame your B2B business: range
breadth vs. depth, direct vs. reseller share, margin corridor
per customer class.
emphasis.market:
B2B market = a few large players, longer sales cycles. Capture
the main competitors + their USPs (terms, range, lead time).
Industry market share if relevant — growth comes from customer
acquisition + share-of-wallet, not ad pressure.
emphasis.company:
B2B document flow is mandatory — quote → order → delivery note →
invoice. T&C footers, dunning copy, early-payment rules and
delivery terms must be clean. Reverse-charge and third-country
exports are standard, not exception.
emphasis.products:
List price + volume tiers + customer-specific prices (special
terms) + project prices. Margin per customer class explicit —
who gets what at what price and why. Range cut: core range
(always in stock) vs. specials (made-to-order) vs. drop-ship.
emphasis.masterdata:
Master data is the core of your business — customer groups +
price lists + terms + credit limits + payment terms. Clean
customer classes are the prerequisite for correct pricing and
dunning automation.
emphasis.channels:
Share of direct sales (field reps) vs. inside sales vs. optional
B2B shop. NO marketplace play. EDI integrations for top
customers when relevant (EDIFACT, OpenTrans, REST).
emphasis.processes:
Quote-to-cash with approval workflow for special prices and
credit-limit overrides. SLA per order type (rush, stock,
drop-ship). Complaint process instead of returns portal.
emphasis.payment:
Payment terms 14/30/60/90 days net, early-payment discount
rules, prepayment thresholds for new customers, credit limit
per customer + automatic block on overage. SEPA direct debit
when mandates are in place.
emphasis.accounting:
Dunning + open-item analysis are daily business. Mandatory:
EC sales list monthly for EU-B2B (reverse-charge), third-country
export proof, VAT pre-registration. Factoring or credit
insurance as risk hedge to consider.
emphasis.roles:
Approval workflow for special prices and credit-limit
increases. Field reps see only their own customers + commission
base. Four-eyes principle above thresholds.
emphasis.custom_agents:
Dunning levels automated and time-triggered, open-item reports
to accounting weekly, credit-limit check before order release,
commission settlement monthly.
emphasis.logistics:
Mix of parcel (carrier API) and freight (pallet, FTL). Per-order
freight logic. Warehouse picking more batch/wave for large
volumes, less single-order. Drop-ship as bypass.
emphasis.systems:
ERP as the central system — connected to accounting (DATEV),
optionally PIM, optionally CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive),
EDI provider and credit-rating service (Creditreform).
emphasis.financials:
Customer concentration risk + DSO + open-item balance are the
KPIs. Margin waterfall per customer class. Cash-flow forecast
from payment terms + expected early-payment discounts.
emphasis.reporting:
Top-30 customer report, open items, DSO, dunning-level
pipeline, commission analysis. Per field rep their own view on
their customers + pipeline.
emphasis.migration:
Cut-over plan with top customers first → mid-tier → long tail.
Test EDI mappings before go-live. Migrate balances cleanly so
credit-limit check works from day 1.

tasks.title: Top-30 customer list with terms
tasks.hint: Customer no. · payment term · early-payment discount · credit limit · rep · price group

tasks.title: Price lists + volume tiers
tasks.hint: List price · tiers per customer group · specials flag

tasks.title: Customer-specific prices (special terms)
tasks.hint: Per top customer Excel with SKU + special price + validity

tasks.title: Document framework contracts
tasks.hint: Volume · call-off quantity · term · price lock · termination rules

tasks.title: Credit-rating & credit-limit policy
tasks.hint: Default limit · special releases · Creditreform integration · block rules

tasks.title: Configure dunning
tasks.hint: 3 levels + dunning copy + dunning fees + delivery stop from level 2

tasks.title: Approval workflow for special prices
tasks.hint: Who may approve up to what margin / threshold — written + in system

tasks.title: Field-rep setup + commission model
tasks.hint: User accounts · customer assignment · commission rate · settlement frequency

tasks.title: EDI integration top customers
tasks.hint: GLN · EDIFACT/OpenTrans · mapping table · test order

tasks.title: Bank login (FinAPI/EBICS) for open items
tasks.hint: Auto-match · dunning-level trigger · batch postings

tasks.title: Factoring / credit insurance
tasks.hint: Contract · limit file format · communication workflow on limit reduction

tasks.title: DATEV tax-advisor access + EC sales list
tasks.hint: Advisor no · client no · EC sales list frequency monthly for EU-B2B

tasks.title: Test users sales / inside / accounting
tasks.hint: Training instance · approval test · dunning-level drill

tasks.title: Roll-out plan top customers first
tasks.hint: Which top customer live from when · hypercare plan · escalation contacts
